purpose: Give the next agent a clear source-download starting point.

overall_recommendation.source: Colorado Governor's Office or State Archives
overall_recommendation.reason: The authenticated LegiScan refresh completed on July 6, 2026. The remaining highest-priority download issue is EO-2019-007, where the official public link returns a sign-in page instead of a usable PDF.

safety_gate.current_state: The July 2 checkpoint is committed and pushed on codex/july-2-corpus-checkpoint. It still needs pull-request review and merge before it is public-facing on main.
required_before_large_download: Project owner completes docs/PUBLICATION_CHECKLIST.md before public-facing merge, or
Project owner explicitly authorizes proceeding with the next download before merging the checkpoint branch.

next_actions.why_it_matters: This watcher checks each official source before future downloads and separates safe guarded downloads from manual source-review work.
next_actions.ready_when: Run before every broad source refresh or scheduled download window.
follow_up_after_success: Use _CONTROL_PLANE/SOURCE_UPDATE_DOWNLOAD_QUEUE.json for the next guarded download or manual review item.
Do not download from a source unless the watcher, source registry, and safety checkpoint agree.

next_actions.why_it_matters: This refresh updates current Colorado bill data and keeps the legislation layer from drifting behind the official source.
follow_up_after_success: Commit and push the refreshed legislation files.
Use the next routine refresh window for future LegiScan updates.
Keep the API key outside repository files.

next_actions.blocked_by: The official public link returns a Google Drive sign-in page instead of a usable PDF.
next_actions.why_it_matters: This is the only known failed executive order source artifact from the July 2 executive order rebuild.
next_actions.ready_when: A valid official copy is received from the Governor's Office, State Archives, or another approved official transfer.
next_actions.suggested_command_after_authorization: python -m geode.pipeline.manual_source_intake --root . --apply --record-id EO-2019-007 --layer-id 05_Executive_Orders --source-file <official_pdf_path> --official-source-name "Colorado Governor's Office or State Archives" --official-source-url https://www.colorado.gov/governor/2019-executive-orders --acquisition-method state_archives_request --received-from <source_contact> --reviewer-name <reviewer_name> --custody-note <custody_note> --json
follow_up_after_success: Rerun the executive order source-anchoring workflow.
Rebuild the executive order layer outputs.

next_actions.why_it_matters: The 41 modern-year permanent LegiScan document gaps are small enough to repair before the large pre-2018 legacy archive project.
next_actions.ready_when: The project owner authorizes a focused source repair pass for the modern LegiScan queue.
follow_up_after_success: Confirm the modern_year_failed_permanent count drops in _CONTROL_PLANE/SOURCE_LIMITATION_REGISTER.json.

next_actions.why_it_matters: Some rulemaking records can be strengthened by attaching eDocket detail pages and supporting documents where available.
next_actions.ready_when: The project owner chooses to improve source depth after the higher-priority blocked refreshes are handled.
next_actions.suggested_command_after_authorization: Review _CONTROL_PLANE/SOURCE_REPAIR_DASHBOARD.json, then run the register or eDocket source-depth workflow selected by the project owner.
follow_up_after_success: Rerun source strength and relationship accuracy reports.
Confirm no new repair queue items were created.

do_not_start_next: Do not start a broad all-source refresh until the safety gate is cleared.
Do not use unofficial replacement files for EO-2019-007.
Do not store the LegiScan API key in repository files.
boundary: This dashboard is an operations guide. It does not authorize legal reliance, source substitution, or unsupervised use.
